candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: This appears directly beneath the selected COGS total ('Cost of sales' / 'Cost Of Sales') as a component within cost of revenues, so it is already embedded in the selected COGS bucket and should not be added separately.

This is presented as a sub-component of the selected COGS total ('Cost of sales' / 'Cost Of Sales'), indicating it is already included in that bucket rather than a separate same-level operating line to add later.
This row is blank in the excerpt and does not provide a usable separate operating D&A amount for COGS.
This row is blank and appears generic rather than a clear separate COGS D&A line.

variables.notes: Selected COGS references are total cost-of-sales rows. The visible COGS-related D&A rows are nested components within those totals, so adding them separately would double count.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles in Sales and distribution expenses
candidates.why: This sits under the selected SG&A parent row 'Sales and distribution expenses' and therefore appears already embedded in the selected XSGA bucket.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation in Sales and distribution expenses
candidates.why: This is a component of the selected 'Sales and distribution expenses' total, so it should not be added separately.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles in Administrative expenses
candidates.why: This appears as a sub-component of the selected 'Administrative expenses' row and is therefore already included in the selected XSGA bucket.
This is presented beneath the selected administrative expense total and looks embedded in that parent row, so adding it separately would double count.
This row is blank and not a usable separate same-level SG&A D&A row.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA references are the operating expense totals (sales/distribution, administrative, etc.). The visible SG&A D&A rows are clearly component lines within those selected totals, not separate same-level rows to add later. No separate R&D amortization row is shown.
